name: Lock In Strava authenticated GPS activity
description: Proves a logged-in Strava athlete and their most recent Run using canonical activity id, title, UTC start time, distance, GPS, trainer, Strava flag, and signed motion metrics. The run is bound to the Lock by the on-chain day window and the global activity nullifier, not by its title.

runtimePolicy.activitySelection: models[0], the most recent Run returned by Strava for this athlete
runtimePolicy.bindingModel: Binding comes from the escrow, not from the activity title: joinPact closes at pact.startsAt so every participant is committed before day 1; the verifier requires start_time inside the day window [pact.startsAt + dayIndex*1d, +1d); usedEventNullifiers is global over the activity id so one run can never be counted twice; identityOwner[pactId][identityHash] gives one Strava identity one participant per Lock; the athlete marker proves the run is the caller's.

requestData.purpose: Bind evidence to the currently authenticated Strava athlete
Read the athlete's most recent Run and its signed metrics.

customInjection.purpose: Diagnostic self-reporting injection: drives the training and challenge-filtered activity GET requests after sign-in (activities request sends Accept: application/json and X-Requested-With), and if it cannot release within the window it calls reclaim.reportProviderError with the exact stage, host, /athlete/training status, training_activities status and matched title so the hosted-flow failure point is visible. Reads only the daily proof code; never reads or exports cookies.

authoringEvidence.requestSchemaChange: dropped the {{context_challenge}} keyword from the activity URL and removed the context_challenge parameter; the activity is now selected as the athlete's most recent Run instead of by title
authoringEvidence.reasonForChange: requiring the user to retitle their Strava run with a 30-character code is not a viable consumer flow, and the code was redundant against the escrow's own bindings
authoringEvidence.publishedProviderVersionOnReclaim: pending: publish 7.0.0 on the Reclaim dashboard
authoringEvidence.previousVersionLiveEvidence: 6.0.0 reached PROOF_SUBMITTED with verificationType WITNESS and both request hashes matching policy
